notes: Chunk 73 DONE (Template C, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block), a new Tier B cluster: EU/other high-value origins into Mexico/Brazil/Malaysia/Indonesia/South Korea, plus Slovenia's full reciprocal gap with those same five countries. This is the first chunk since 61-72 to move off the Baltic/Caucasus/Lebanon/Rwanda/Caribbean cluster, which chunk 72 confirmed was exhausted. Group 1, Mexico destination (6): Netherlands/Switzerland/India/Italy/Portugal/Slovenia to Mexico. Mexico's SENASICA import process carries no advance permit (SENASICA clears at the port of entry; older route files' 'SAGARPA permit' framing is outdated) and no titre test, since Mexico is EU-listed low-rabies-risk (verified against Annex II to Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/636). Group 2, Brazil destination (3): Switzerland/India/Slovenia to Brazil. Corrected a live inconsistency found across existing Brazil route files (some claimed a 30-day mandatory quarantine, others none): verified via MAPA's own guidance that Brazil requires no quarantine, no import permit, a 30-day wait after a first rabies vaccination, and no titre test; flagged the older conflicting pages for a future correction pass. Group 3, Malaysia destination (4): Switzerland/Italy/Portugal/Slovenia to Malaysia, DVS process, FAVN titre test, 90-day wait, 14-day quarantine, breed ban on Pit Bull/American Staffordshire/Staffordshire Bull Terrier/Bull Terrier/Rottweiler and crosses. Group 4, Indonesia destination (4): Switzerland/India/Portugal/Slovenia to Indonesia, Barantin rekomendasi two-stage permit, FAVN titre 0.5 IU/ml, 7-14 day quarantine, no direct Bali entry. Group 5, South Korea destination (3): Portugal/Greece/Slovenia to South Korea, QIAS process, no titre test for EU origins but a 1-3 day QIAS inspection hold at Incheon (not zero as some older South Korea files imply; flagged for review). Group 6, Slovenia destination (5): Mexico/Brazil/Malaysia/Indonesia/South Korea to Slovenia, the reverse direction. Safety-critical correction made during QA: the build agent initially classified Mexico and Malaysia as EU-unlisted (titre test required) for the Slovenia-destination files, based on apparent internal site precedent; independently verified against two sources (EU food-safety listing page and BMLEH's published country list) that Mexico and Malaysia are in fact EU-listed low-rabies-risk countries, so mexico-to-slovenia.md and malaysia-to-slovenia.md were rewritten in full to remove the titre test/90-day wait requirement and shorten the estimated timeline from 18-22 to 5-8 weeks; Brazil, Indonesia and South Korea remain correctly unlisted (titre test plus 90-day wait applies). All 25 files verified structurally (valid YAML, requirements blocks are keyed maps, template_variant C corrected on 2 files that were written as D/B, Marcus Webb byline) and QA-scanned for zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary, zero curly quotes, zero dead /pet-transport/routes/ paths, and no email/phone mentions; no exact-sentence overlap between sibling files exceeded 7%, well under the 15% shared-copy reject threshold. Two known legacy data-quality issues flagged for a future correction pass, not fixed in this chunk: (1) existing Brazil-destination route files disagree on the quarantine requirement, (2) existing Mexico-destination route files disagree on whether SENASICA requires an advance import permit. Routes 6,246 -> 6,271, pages 7,050 -> 7,075. Chunk 74 (Template D, Tier B) is next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
